summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering a proclamation for Congenital Heart Defect Awareness Week, approved the 2022 budget true-up, scheduled the annual cleanup day for June 4, hired eight part-time firefighter/EMT personnel, approved police training and credit card authorization changes, and held a public hearing on zoning case ZC-2021-007 for a preliminary development plan (PDP) at 6600 Patricia Boulevard for a platform tennis court manufacturing business. Residents voiced significant concerns about truck traffic, child safety, notification procedures, and the appropriateness of commercial use in a residential area, while the zoning director defended the township's notification and procedural compliance.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – approved
Motion to approve payment of bills (Jan 12–25, 2022) – approved
Proclamation declaring February 7–14, 2022 as Congenital Heart Defect Awareness Week – approved
Motion to approve 2022 budget true-up for revenue and appropriations – approved
Motion to approve cleanup day for June 4, 2022 – approved
Motion to appoint part-time firefighter paramedic Cody Bryant at $17/hr effective Jan 30, 2022 – approved
Motion to appoint part-time firefighter EMT Stephen Hill at $15.50/hr effective Jan 30, 2022 – approved
Motion to appoint part-time firefighter EMT Nathan Denaldin at $15.50/hr effective Jan 30, 2022 – approved
Motion to appoint part-time firefighter EMT Joshua Ulrich at $15.50/hr effective Jan 30, 2022 – approved
Motion to appoint part-time firefighter EMT Aaron Walsh at $15.50/hr effective Jan 30, 2022 – approved
Motion to appoint part-time firefighter EMT Clayton Newman at $15.50/hr effective Jan 30, 2022 – approved
Motion to appoint part-time firefighter EMT Adam Fani at $15.50/hr effective Jan 30, 2022 – approved
Motion to appoint part-time firefighter EMT Elizabeth Hyland at $15.50/hr effective Jan 30, 2022 – approved
Motion to approve Officer Chris McMillan to attend sexual assault investigations training ($230) – approved
Motion to approve Officer Justin Porterfield to attend criminal drug interdiction training ($350) – approved
Motion to add Officer Laura Item as authorized township credit card user – approved
Motion to remove Officer Kristen Groves as authorized township credit card user – approved
action_items: Administrator Peacock to forward ARC stimulus fund spending list to trustees and place on future agenda for discussion
Cheryl and Chad to coordinate with service department and contractors for June 4 cleanup day
Harry Holbert to prepare BZA resolution for VGA case 2021-10 conditional use approval next month
Applicant (Mark Keeby) to incorporate board conditions into PDP and resubmit for FDP approval if approved

key_amounts: $7,000 for police car insurance repair (vehicle damaged early January 2022)
$17/hr rate for part-time firefighter paramedic Cody Bryant
$15.50/hr rate for seven part-time firefighter EMTs
$230 for Officer McMillan sexual assault investigations training
$350 for Officer Porterfield criminal drug interdiction training
$12.7 million total 2022 budget including $1.7 million in ARC stimulus funds (~$10 million without stimulus)
$114,000 general fund transfer to zoning in 2021 reduced to ~$46,000 in 2022
Over $300,000 carried over from 2021 into 2022 savings
Under $10 million ARC threshold confirmed allowing flexible spending on infrastructure
notable_topics: Congenital Heart Defect Awareness Week proclamation (Feb 7-14, 2022)
Annual cleanup day scheduled for June 4, 2022
Police officer training approvals and credit card authorization updates
American Recovery Plan (ARC) stimulus fund spending clarification from prosecutor's office
New plow truck delivered and displayed at Ohio Township Conference
Ambulance loaned to Madera Indian Department Fire District
Zoning case ZC-2021-007 public hearing for PDP/FDP at 6600 Patricia Boulevard (Total Platform Tennis)
Resident concerns about truck traffic safety near Kinder Care daycare and residential streets
Zoning notification procedures and public records request concerns
VGA case 2021-10 conditional use for athletic turf fields and field house approved
attendees: Ron Rossetti (property owner, 104 Marshall Drive, Pennsylvania)
Robin Desmuk (Kinder Care Learning Centers district manager)
notes: The public hearing on zoning case ZC-2021-007 was highly contentious with multiple residents objecting to commercial use on Patricia Boulevard. Dr. Roark delivered extensive testimony questioning procedural compliance, notification failures, and the wisdom of allowing light manufacturing in a residential area. The zoning director defended notification procedures and clarified that the withdrawn zone change case (ZC-2021-003) was separate from the PDP/FDP review. The transcript appears truncated before the board's final decision on the zoning case. Ron Rossetti, who drove five hours from Pennsylvania, is the landowner of the adjacent 30-acre B2-zoned parcel and supported the application. The new plow truck was displayed at the Ohio Township Association conference in Columbus before delivery to Goshen.
